comment: Crowd identity trait pools for Hesperus Market. Loaded by crowd_director.gd. The contract generator (post-sprint) samples from files like this — add districts by adding files, not code.
comment_funnel: PHASE: single scripted bounty (Option B). target_in_crowd=false -> the crowd is PURE DECOYS, no is_target NPC. funnel_profile is the courtyard Korvaxi's known traits (read from his sprite); it drives clue values + witness hints so the funnel narrows toward HIM, but the only valid scan/confront is the scripted courtyard model itself. Candidates share his build and differ in >=1 trait. When the generator takes over: set target_in_crowd=true (or delete these keys) and it rolls a hidden-in-crowd target again.
comment_authored: PHASE E (05 doc): authored_candidates is the HAND-BUILT decoy field for this single contract — the generator's Venn-overlap step done by hand. Target visible profile = red coat / heavy gait / courtyard. Field is sized so: (a) full conjunction red+heavy+courtyard isolates ONLY the triple-twin decoy (#1), forcing the scanner signature to separate it from the real courtyard target; (b) no single visible trait isolates 1 (red on 5, heavy on 6, courtyard on 5); (c) #1 shares ALL visible traits and differs only in signature; (d) no decoy looks more wanted than the answer. Counts are the manual test fixture the overlap algorithm must reproduce later. Each entry overrides only what differs; omitted visible traits default to the target's value, signature defaults to 'clean'.

authored_candidates._note: #1 TRIPLE TWIN: shares all visible traits; ONLY the signature separates him from the real target. Proves the analysis gate.
#8 second red+heavy so that pair isn't unique
#9 pure noise candidate: shares only build
#10 second heavy+courtyard so that pair isn't unique
